They have certain price points that they will not go past. And so your conversation, constantly with people, and your conversation with, if you're in business, if you consult a coach, your conversation will incorporate statements made by the clients that, oh, our customers would never spend X, whatever that amount is. Our customers would never spend more than Y on this. Oh, customers in Beverly Hills might spend that much for this, but customers in Dufus, West Virginia would never spend that much for this. All of these predetermined ideas about money. And they are all limiting statements. A client who is highly skilled, highly talented, all of the elements we would look for to be fabulously successful in the consulting coaching business. They should probably be doing 30 million a year. They're really doing about five. And if you strung them all together from a day, you would have about three hours of these limiting statements about what their customers won't do and why their customers won't pay X and all of this. Some years ago, I empirically proved to them that you could do pretty much what Ron Carothers did. That you could take their $1,000 price point and you could make it, say, $15,000 price point, and it mattered not. And so they've left it at $15,000. And have continued that part of their business. But all the other parts of their business, they're still back where they were before, of our customers will never spend more than this. Oh, my God, our customers would never spend that much. And the 412 reasons why their customers wouldn't spend, including the fact that the customers don't have the money. And the best illustration of that is to, when you go home, go find the worst mobile home park in your community you can find. I mean, you want them, you know, the kids with no underwear on run around out in the front gravel in front of the parking thing and you want them up on blocks, lean into one side, did a couple of good crack dealers in there. And I mean, that kind of mobile home park where they are just falling apart. There will be satellite televisions. There will be dishes mounted on the sides to go to the 52-inch television set inside the mobile home, which actually the weight of the thing is knocking the mobile home off of its blocks. Because people have the money for what they really wish to spend the money on, or what they decide to spend the money on. And almost without exception. We will underestimate their ability and willingness to spend. Now the nice thing about our businesses, most of you, well, almost everybody in here, probably their coaching and consulting, has a direct ROI result to it. I mean, our businesses increase their capacity to spend the more they buy. If they use it at all. Because they use it, they do better. Well, then they can buy more. They use it, they do better, they can buy more. And we should be thinking in terms of that cycle. The other interesting thing about this first, several years I spoke at Joe Polish's boot camps for carpet cleaners. And there were all, every year, we did hot seats. And every year, after the first year, I started making fun of it ahead of time, thinking that that would end it, but it never did. There would always be somebody who would come up with their hot seat. And their contention was that all of their customers were cheapskates. And everybody in their community was poor. And everybody bought by price. And because competitors did it at two cents a square foot, they had to do it at two cents a square foot. By the way, as an aside, I hope you noticed the one getting the most money in Feng Shui had a pricing formula. Rather than quoting a fee, she had a mechanism for figuring out how much the fee should be, which in that case would have been $7,000 for a bail charge. Useful lesson. And so I would sit there in a hot seat and I would say, well, if that be true about where you live, then there should only be one store. Walmart. That's it. There should be no other stores. Because if everybody in your community buys everything by price, you can't be Walmart's price and they sell everything.
